title: Trust Registration Online in India | Process, Cost & Documents

hero.headline: Legally Register Your Trust with Expert Support
hero.subtext: Get your charitable or religious trust registered with ease. Professionals Law handles deed drafting, legal filing, 12A/80G, and post-registration compliance end-to-end.

introduction.heading: Introduction and Its Compliance
body: A Trust is a legal and non-profit organization formed with the objective to promote a charitable, religious, educational or social cause in India. Governed by the Indian Trusts Act, 1882, a settlor drafts a Trust Deed and transfers the property to trustees who would hold it on behalf of the beneficiaries or a cause. Trusts can either be public or private, based on their objectives. At Professionals Law, we help individuals, families and institutions to form Trusts by drafting a Trust Deed for them, assisting in all the legal formalities and registering the Trust with the relevant authorities, including facilitating the 12A and 80G certification processes for tax exemption.

whyNeeded.heading: Why It Is Needed
body: Registering a Trust provides a recognized, legal structure to operate the charitable or religious function. It ensures transparency, credibility with donors and offers eligibility for government grants and tax exemptions. Professionals Law does everything from the start to end to make your philanthropic endeavours become a legal reality.

body.title: Tax Benefits
body.description: Registered Trusts can avail income tax exemptions under Sections 12A and 80G, making them financially viable and attractive to donors who seek deductions.

body.title: Legal Recognition
body.description: A registered Trust has a legal identity, allowing it to hold property, enter into contracts and operate independently.

body.title: Structured Governance
body.description: Trust Deeds outline roles, responsibilities and objectives, ensuring transparent governance and smooth administration.

body.title: Public Trust and Credibility
body.description: A Registration enhances the reputation of the Trust, builds confidence among beneficiaries, donors and government institutions.

body.title: Access to Government Schemes
body.description: A Registered Trusts can apply for government aid, CSR funding and welfare project collaborations.

body.title: Perpetual Succession
body.description: A Trust remains operational even with a change in trustees, ensuring long-term sustainability of charitable activities.

criteria: Any individual or group of individuals can form a Trust.
A minimum of two trustees is recommended, though more can be appointed.
The Trust Deed must define clear charitable objectives.
Trust property must be clearly identified in the Trust Deed.
Foreign nationals or entities can also create Trusts with proper FCRA compliance, if applicable.

documents: PAN Card and Aadhaar of settlor and trustees (KYC documents)
Electricity bill/utility bill for registered address
Ownership proof or No Objection Certificate (NOC) from the property owner
Trust Deed drafted on non-judicial stamp paper
ID and address proof of witnesses
Professionals Law ensures proper document collection, drafting and filing to avoid delays or rejections.

body.description: Professionals Law understands your objectives and recommends the best structure and type of Trust.
Customised drafting of the Trust Deed outlining objectives, trustees and rules.
Procurement of stamp paper of appropriate value based on the trust property value and state regulations.
Submitting the Trust Deed at the local Registrar's office with required documents.
Physical presence of trustees may be required for identity verification.
Post-verification, a registration certificate is issued.
Assistance with PAN, 12A, 80G registration and opening of a Trust bank account.

penalties.heading: Penalties
body: Failure to register the Trust may result in ineligibility for tax exemptions and legal recognition.
Mismanagement or deviation from objectives can lead to cancellation of the Trust’s registration.
Inaccurate financial reporting may attract penalties under the Income Tax Act.
Not maintaining proper books of accounts and annual filings could lead to fines and scrutiny from authorities.

body.description: Trust registration typically takes 15 to 25 working days, depending on document readiness, registrar availability and complexity of objectives. Professionals Law works efficiently to minimize delays and ensure smooth processing.

body.question: Is registration of a Trust mandatory in India?
body.answer: While private Trusts may operate without registration, public charitable Trusts must be registered to avail legal benefits and tax exemptions.

body.question: What is the minimum number of trustees required?
body.answer: A minimum of two trustees is generally required, though more can be appointed as per the Trust Deed.

body.question: Can a Trust earn income?
body.answer: Yes, but the income must be used solely for the Trust’s charitable or religious objectives.

body.question: What is the validity of a registered Trust?
body.answer: Once registered, a Trust continues to exist until dissolved as per the terms mentioned in the Trust Deed.

body.question: What is the benefit of 12A and 80G registration?
body.answer: 12A exempts the Trust’s income from tax, while 80G allows donors to claim tax deductions on their donations.

body.question: Can a Trust operate in multiple states?
body.answer: Yes, a Trust can work nationwide if such a clause is included in the Trust Deed.

body.question: Is physical presence required during registration?
body.answer: In most states, the settlor and two trustees must be physically present before the registrar.

body.question: Can a Trust be modified after registration?
body.answer: Yes, changes to trustees or objectives can be made through a supplementary deed and subsequent legal formalities.

body.question: Can Professionals Law assist with post-registration compliance?
body.answer: Yes, Professionals Law provides end-to-end services, including PAN, bank account setup and tax registrations.
